audit_status.source_base: Greek Romans text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for judging reader, impartial judgment, Law/nations, true circumcision, inward Judean identity. Anti-Jewish misuse must be avoided.

verses.restored: Therefore you are without excuse, O human, everyone who judges; for in what you judge the other, you condemn yourself, for you who judge practice the same things.
We know that the judgment of the Divine is according to truth upon those practicing such things.
Do you calculate this, O human, you who judge those practicing such things and do them, that you will escape the judgment of the Divine?
Or do you despise the riches of his kindness, restraint, and patience, not knowing that the kindness of the Divine leads you into turning/realignment?
But according to your hardness and unturned heart, you store up wrath for yourself in a day of wrath and revelation of the righteous/just judgment of the Divine,
who will give back to each according to one's works:
to those who by endurance in good work seek glory and honor and incorruptibility, age-long life;
but to those from selfish ambition, disobeying the truth and obeying injustice, wrath and fury.
Pressure and distress upon every soul/life of a human working harm, Judean first and also Greek;
but glory and honor and peace to everyone working good, Judean first and also Greek.
For there is no partiality with the Divine.
For as many as sinned/misaligned without Law will also perish without Law, and as many as sinned/misaligned in Law will be judged through Law.
For not the hearers of Law are righteous/just before the Divine, but the doers of Law will be made righteous/just.
For when nations who do not have Law by nature do the things of the Law, these, not having Law, are Law to themselves.
They show the work of the Law written in their hearts, their conscience bearing witness together, and their thoughts between one another accusing or even defending,
on the day when the Divine judges the hidden things of humans according to my good news through Messiah Jesus.
But if you call yourself Judean and rest upon Law and boast in the Divine,
and know the will and approve the things that differ, being instructed from the Law,
and are persuaded that you yourself are guide of blind ones, light of those in darkness,
educator of uncomprehending ones, teacher of infants, having the embodiment of knowledge and truth in the Law—
you therefore who teach another, do you not teach yourself? You who proclaim not to steal, do you steal?
You who say not to commit adultery, do you commit adultery? You who detest idols, do you rob temples?
You who boast in Law, through transgression of the Law do you dishonor the Divine?
For the name of the Divine is blasphemed among the nations because of you, just as it is written.
For circumcision indeed profits if you practice Law; but if you are a transgressor of Law, your circumcision has become uncircumcision.
If therefore the uncircumcision guards the righteous/just requirements of the Law, will not that one's uncircumcision be counted as circumcision?
And the uncircumcision by nature, completing the Law, will judge you who through letter and circumcision are transgressor of Law.
For the one outwardly is not Judean, nor is circumcision outward in flesh;
but the one inwardly is Judean, and circumcision is of heart, in Spirit, not letter, whose praise is not from humans but from the Divine.

safety_note: Romans 2 turns the critique of Romans 1 onto the judging reader. It must not be used as anti-Jewish replacement rhetoric; Paul's argument is intra-Jewish, Torah-literate, and aimed at hypocrisy, impartial judgment, and inward covenant faithfulness.
